info.title: EasyLend API
info.description: Core Backend for the EasyLend Practice Enterprise

post.description:
Validate an NFC badge before PIN verification.

Public endpoint for the kiosk sign-in flow.
Rate-limited: 500 req/min per IP (Layer 2).

schema.title: Response Nfc Login Api V1 Auth Nfc Post

post.description:
Verify the PIN and issue access and refresh tokens.

Public endpoint for the kiosk sign-in flow.
Rate-limited: 500 req/min per IP (Layer 2).

Rotate a valid refresh token and return a new token pair.

Public endpoint for authenticated kiosk sessions.
Rate-limited: 500 req/min per IP (Layer 2).

Revoke the provided refresh token and end the active session.

Public endpoint and idempotent by design.

schema.title: Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description:
Return all roles that can be assigned to users.

Requires Admin role. No request body is required.

schema.title: Response List Roles Api V1 Roles Get

get.description:
List users with pagination.

Requires Admin role.

parameters.name: skip

schema.description: Number of users to skip before returning results (pagination offset).
schema.title: Skip

parameters.description: Number of users to skip before returning results (pagination offset).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of users to return in a single response (pagination page size).
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of users to return in a single response (pagination page size).

post.description:
Create a new user account.

Requires Admin role.

get.description:
Return the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Requires authentication.

Return a user by unique identifier.

Requires Admin role.

patch.description:
Partially update an existing user.

Requires Admin role.

post.description:
Anonymize a user account (GDPR Right to be Forgotten).

Replaces all PII fields (first_name, last_name, email, nfc_tag_id) with
non-identifying placeholder values and replaces pin_hash with a hash of a
randomly generated credential, effectively disabling login with the original PIN.
Also sets status=UserStatus.ANONYMIZED.
Writes an audit log entry with action_type="USER_ANONYMIZED".

Requires Admin role.

patch.description:
Update the NFC tag linked to a user account.

Requires Admin role.

get.description:
Export all personal data for a user (GDPR Right to Portability).

Accessible by the user themselves OR an Admin. Raises 403 otherwise.

schema.title: Response Export User Data Api V1 Users  User Id  Export Get

get.description:
List all asset categories with pagination.

Accessible by every authenticated user (student, medewerker, admin).
Used by the kiosk catalog view to group available assets.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

post.description:
Create a new asset category.

Requires Admin role. `category_name` must be unique (DB constraint).

patch.description:
Update an existing category.

Requires Admin role. All fields in the request body are optional so
partial updates are supported.

get.description:
List all registered kiosk devices with pagination.

Requires Admin role.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

post.description:
Register a new kiosk device.

Requires Admin role. Status defaults to `ONLINE` unless explicitly
set otherwise in the request body.

get.description:
List all lockers that belong to a specific kiosk, ordered by slot number.

Accessible by every authenticated user. Used by both the kiosk boot
sequence (JWT Service Account) and the Admin Locker Grid UI (JWT Admin token).
Returns a 404 if the kiosk does not exist.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

patch.description:
Update a kiosk's operational status.

Requires Admin role. Typically used to change `kiosk_status`
(e.g. `ONLINE` → `MAINTENANCE`) via the Admin Remote Control flow.

get.description:
List all lockers across all kiosks with pagination.

Requires Admin role.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

post.description:
Add a new locker to an existing kiosk.

Requires Admin role. `kiosk_id` must reference an existing kiosk.
`logical_number` is the physical slot label (1, 2, 3 …) and must be
unique within the kiosk.

get.description:
Retrieve a single locker by its unique identifier.

Requires Admin role.

post.description:
Force-open a locker door via the Vision Box WebSocket.

Requires Admin role. Sends an `open_slot` WSS command to the kiosk that
owns the locker and writes an `ADMIN_FORCED_OPEN` audit log entry.
Returns 503 if the Vision Box for the associated kiosk is offline.
Idempotency: requires Idempotency-Key header. Duplicate keys return 409.

schema.title: Response Force Open Locker Api V1 Lockers  Locker Id  Open Post

503.description: Vision Box offline for this kiosk

patch.description:
Update a locker's operational status.

Requires Admin role. Primarily used by the Quarantine flow to set
`locker_status = MAINTENANCE` when a damage inspection is pending,
or to restore a locker to `AVAILABLE` after an admin resolves it.

Payload must include a locker_status value.

get.description:
Role-aware catalog view.

- Admins: return one row per asset including active/reserved loan context.
- Non-admins: return grouped counts per category of AVAILABLE assets.

schema.title: Response Get Catalog Api V1 Catalog Get

get.description:
List assets with pagination and an optional status filter.

Accessible by every authenticated user. Soft-deleted assets
(`is_deleted = True`) are always excluded from this listing so
that the catalog never surfaces retired equipment.

Use the `asset_status` query parameter to narrow results:
- Kiosk app catalog view: `?asset_status=AVAILABLE`
- Admin overview: omit the filter to see everything active.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

parameters.name: asset_status

schema.description: Optional filter. Only return assets with this status. Possible values: AVAILABLE, BORROWED, RESERVED, PENDING_INSPECTION, MAINTENANCE, LOST.
schema.title: Asset Status

parameters.description: Optional filter. Only return assets with this status. Possible values: AVAILABLE, BORROWED, RESERVED, PENDING_INSPECTION, MAINTENANCE, LOST.

post.description:
Register a new physical asset in the system.

Requires Admin role.

- `aztec_code` must be unique (matches the barcode printed on the item).
- `category_id` must reference an existing category.
- `locker_id` is optional; omit it if the asset is not yet physically
  placed in a locker (e.g. asset is being prepared).

400.description: Duplicate aztec_code, invalid category_id, or invalid locker_id

get.description:
Retrieve a single asset by its unique identifier.

Accessible by every authenticated user. Returns the asset regardless
of `is_deleted` status so that historical records remain retrievable
when the ID is known.

patch.description:
Update an existing asset.

Requires Admin role. All fields in `AssetUpdate` are optional so
partial updates are supported.

Common admin use-cases:
- Mark an asset `MAINTENANCE` or `LOST` via `asset_status`.
- Re-assign an asset to a different category or locker.
- Correct a typo in `name` or `aztec_code`.

`is_deleted` is deliberately **not** updatable here: use the
dedicated `DELETE /{asset_id}` endpoint for soft-deletion.

400.description: Duplicate aztec_code, invalid category_id, or invalid locker_id

delete.description:
Soft-delete an asset (sets `is_deleted = True`).

Requires Admin role. The record is **not** physically removed; all
historical loan records and AI evaluations that reference this asset
remain intact. The asset is hidden from the `GET /assets` listing
immediately after deletion.

`asset_status` is intentionally preserved as-is so that audit logs
and in-flight loans are not silently corrupted.

Returns 409 Conflict if the asset has an ACTIVE or RESERVED loan.

409.description: Asset has active or reserved loans

get.description:
List loans with pagination.

- **Admin**: all loans in the system.
- **Any other role**: only the caller's own loans.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

schema.title: Response List Loans Api V1 Loans Get

get.description:
Retrieve the current status of a single loan.

Intended as a lightweight polling endpoint for the kiosk app.

- **Admin**: may poll any loan.
- **Other roles**: may only poll their own loans (returns 403 otherwise).

post.description:
Begin a checkout by scanning an asset's Aztec barcode.

Uses `SELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT` to guarantee that two concurrent
requests for the same asset cannot both succeed. If the row is already
locked, a **409 Conflict** is returned immediately (no retry).

**State transitions (hardware-free path):**
- `Loan.loan_status`: `RESERVED` (initial status before hardware confirms pickup)
- `Asset.asset_status`: `AVAILABLE` → `BORROWED`
- `Asset.locker_id`: unchanged until Vision confirms the checkout result
- `Locker.locker_status`: unchanged until Vision confirms the locker is empty
- New `Loan` record created with `loan_status = RESERVED`

Rate-limited: 60 req/min per authenticated user (Layer 3).

400.description: Asset unavailable, not found, or has no locker assigned

409.description: Conflict: lock contention or processing error

post.description: Report damage during the immediate post-checkout grace period.

400.description: Invalid state or grace period elapsed

404.description: Loan, asset, locker, or user not found

409.description: Conflict: lock contention or duplicate request

post.description:
Initiate the return process for an active loan.

Finds and locks the first available locker at the kiosk where the
user is standing (`payload.kiosk_id`) using `SELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT`
to fail fast if the slot is currently in use by another transaction.

**State transitions:**
- `Locker.locker_status`: `AVAILABLE` → `OCCUPIED` (reserved for this return)
- `Loan.return_locker_id`: assigned to the chosen locker
- `Loan.loan_status`: `ACTIVE` → `RETURNING`

Rate-limited: 60 req/min per authenticated user (Layer 3).

400.description: Loan is not in ACTIVE state

409.description: Conflict: loan state changed or lock contention

503.description: No available lockers at the requested kiosk

schema.title: Response Analyze Image Api V1 Vision Analyze Post

get.description: List all loans in PENDING_INSPECTION status with joined relation names.

schema.title: Response List Quarantine Loans Api V1 Admin Quarantine Get

get.description: Return the most recent AI evaluation for a given loan.

patch.description:
Admin verdict on a quarantined AI evaluation.

- **is_approved == True** (AI was right): Loan → DISPUTED, Asset → MAINTENANCE.
- **is_approved == False** (AI was wrong):
    - CHECKOUT eval: Loan → ACTIVE, Asset → BORROWED, Locker → AVAILABLE.
    - RETURN eval: Loan → COMPLETED, Asset → AVAILABLE, Locker → OCCUPIED.

404.description: Evaluation, loan, asset, or locker not found

get.description: Return a paginated list of audit logs (admin-only).

schema.title: Response List Audit Logs Api V1 Audit  Get

get.description:
Verify the integrity of the audit chain.

Deterministic ordering is enforced via `created_at.asc(), audit_id.asc()`.
The verification walks the chain, checking previous_hash continuity and
recomputing each record's current_hash.

Pagination prevents memory exhaustion when the audit log grows large.
Use `skip` and `limit` to walk the chain in batches; the chain must
be verified contiguously — gaps indicate tampering.
Serve an uploaded image by filename. Enforces strict UUID format and admin access.

AssetCreate.title: AssetCreate
AssetCreate.description: category_id is required; locker_id is optional (asset may be unassigned initially).

AssetUpdate.title: AssetUpdate
AssetUpdate.description:
All fields Optional for PATCH/PUT.

Admins may update any attribute individually, including re-assigning an asset
to a different category or locker, or marking it LOST / MAINTENANCE.
is_deleted is intentionally excluded: use the dedicated soft-delete endpoint.

available_count.title: Available Count
available_count.description: Number of assets in this category that are currently available for checkout.

CategoryCreate.title: CategoryCreate
CategoryCreate.description: No extra fields beyond Base; category_name uniqueness is enforced DB-side.

CheckoutRequest.title: CheckoutRequest
CheckoutRequest.description:
Payload the kiosk sends to initiate a checkout.

The ``aztec_code`` is the barcode scanned from the asset label on the
kiosk screen. The backend resolves the associated asset, uses that
asset's current locker to lock the asset/locker, creates the loan
record, and then frees the locker.

EvaluationDetailView.title: EvaluationDetailView
EvaluationDetailView.description: Most recent AI evaluation for a loan, returned to the admin for judging.

KioskCreate.title: KioskCreate
KioskCreate.description: No extra fields; kiosk_status defaults to ONLINE on creation.

LoanListResponse.title: LoanListResponse
LoanListResponse.description: Paginated list of loans (admin-facing, includes user_id per item).

LoanPublicListResponse.title: LoanPublicListResponse
LoanPublicListResponse.description: Paginated list of loans for regular (non-admin) users — no user_id per item.

LoanPublicResponse.title: LoanPublicResponse
LoanPublicResponse.description:
Loan response WITHOUT user identity — for regular (non-admin) users.

Excludes user_id to prevent IDOR: regular authenticated users must not
learn which other users are associated with specific loans.

LoanResponse.title: LoanResponse
LoanResponse.description:
Full representation of a loan record returned from the API.

Includes user_id — for admin users only.

LoanStatusResponse.title: LoanStatusResponse
LoanStatusResponse.description:
Minimal payload for the status-polling endpoint.

The kiosk polls ``GET /api/v1/loans/{loan_id}/status`` until the
``loan_status`` reaches the desired state for the kiosk flow (for
example, a final state such as ``COMPLETED`` or ``FRAUD_SUSPECTED``).

LockerCreate.title: LockerCreate
LockerCreate.description: kiosk_id is required on creation to assign the locker to a physical kiosk.

rejection_reason.title: Rejection Reason
rejection_reason.description: Required when is_approved is False. Max 255 characters.

QuarantineJudgmentRequest.title: QuarantineJudgmentRequest
QuarantineJudgmentRequest.description: Admin verdict on a quarantined AI evaluation.

QuarantineLoanView.title: QuarantineLoanView
QuarantineLoanView.description: Quarantined loan with joined relation names for the admin dashboard.

ReturnInitiateRequest.title: ReturnInitiateRequest
ReturnInitiateRequest.description:
Payload sent to start the return flow.

The backend resolves the active loan from the scanned ``aztec_code``,
finds a free locker at the kiosk identified by ``kiosk_id`` (the
kiosk where the user is currently standing), and begins the return
process.
